mnemonics.title: IPv4 Header Fields Order
mnemonics.content: Very Impressive To Travel In (Version, IHL, TOS, Total Length, Identification, Flags, Fragment Offset, TTL, Protocol, Header Checksum, Source Address, Destination Address, Options)

mnemonics.title: DHCP DORA Process
mnemonics.content: Do Offer Request Acknowledge - DHCP Discover, Offer, Request, Acknowledgment handshake for IP allocation

mnemonics.title: Routing Algorithm Types
mnemonics.content: D-VLS: Distance Vector (RIP), Link State (OSPF), Vector Distance (EIGRP)

mnemonics.title: Network Layer Functions
mnemonics.content: R-F-F-S: Routing, Forwarding, Fragmentation, Sequencing (delivery order)

acronyms.usage: Maps private internal IPs to public external IPs for internet access sharing
IP addressing scheme using prefix length notation (e.g., /24) instead of classful boundaries
Automatically assigns IP addresses and network configuration to hosts
Error reporting and diagnostic messaging in network layer
Maps IP addresses to MAC addresses on local networks
Prevents packets from circulating indefinitely in the network
Maximum packet size that can be transmitted without fragmentation
Link-state interior gateway protocol using Dijkstra's algorithm

visualTips.visualization: Draw IPv4 header as a row of 32-bit words: Version/IHL (word1), TOS/Total Length (word2), ID/Flags/Offset (word3), TTL/Protocol/Checksum (word4), Source IP (word5), Dest IP (word6), Options (optional words)
Use 8x8 magic box: draw 8x8 grid showing 256 addresses. Each /n adds 2^(8-(n-24)) to network portion. Circle borrowed bits as subnet field.
Show table with Destination, Gateway, Genmask, Flags columns. Draw packet arriving and longest prefix match being performed.
Draw large packet being split at each router showing ID preserved, MF (More Fragments) flag set on all except last fragment, offset recalculated.

analogies.analogy: Like a GPS navigation system: Routing calculates best path (like planning route), Forwarding is like actually driving the car step-by-step at each intersection.
Like a time-limited train ticket: Has certain number of hops (stations) before expiring, each router 'stamps' decrementing value.
Like a building receptionist: Private IPs are internal room numbers, Public IP is building's single phone number; receptionist directs incoming/outgoing calls.
Like postal system: Country (major network), State (subnet), House number (host) - hierarchical addressing for efficient delivery.
Like asking at a party: 'Does anyone know who has IP 192.168.1.5?' - broadcast request to find the person with specific identity.
